Author: Paul Gray
Title: The Rough Guide to Thailand's Beaches and Islands (751906)

Author: Carmen Carter
Title: Star Trek - the Next Generation: Devil's Heart (751962)
ItemDescription: An archaeological outpost has suddenly stopped responding to repeated outside requests for information. When the "Enterprise" crew is sent to look into the matter they discover a devastated outpost and a dying scientist whose last words are: "The devil's heart has been found".

Author: Francesca Duranti
Title: The House on Moon Lake (752103)
ItemDescription: Fabrizio Garrone is an impoverished aristocrat who makes his living through translating and whose cautious existence is marked by his inability to give his heart to anything - not his best friend not his mistress not even his own life. This book won the Bugatta Prize.

Author: Patricia Cornwell
Title: Port Mortuary (752164)
ItemDescription: The latest blockbuster in the bestselling Kay Scarpetta series

Author: DK Publishing
Title: DK Eyewitness Ireland (752563)
ItemDescription: Using the unique 3D models and cutaways this work explores the spectacular scenery of Killarney to the castles of Donegal and Kilkenny and includes tips on where to find the best Ulster Fry and the tastiest Guiness.

Author: Wilbur Smith
Title: A Falcon Flies (752669)
ItemDescription: Set on a trader on the South Atlantic on the fever-ridden shores of the Indian Ocean and in the Eden of the unexplored interior of Africa this wide-sweeping novel describes the Ballantyne family's first conquest of the untamed land of Africa.

Author: Brian Reade
Title: 44 Years with the Same Bird (752785)
ItemDescription: The story of a footballing love affair

Author: Sheila Hutchins
Title: Grannie's Kitchen - Recipes from The North East of England (752847)

Author: Dylan Evans
Title: Placebo (752938)
ItemDescription: A lucid and stimulating explanation of how the body's natural healing mechanisms work - and how they can be triggered in non-chemical ways via the "placebo effect". Evans exposes the flaws in research into the placebo effect and reveals the limits of what can and cannot be cured by thought alone.

Author: Roger Penrose
Title: The Emperor's New Mind (753144)
ItemDescription: In his bestselling work of popular science Sir Roger Penrose takes us on a fascinating roller-coaster ride through the basic principles of physics cosmology mathematics and philosophy to show that human thinking can never be emulated by a machine.

Author: Caroline Behr
Title: Miller's Costume Jewellery (753274)
ItemDescription: Aimed at the new collector this guide takes a look at the leading styles of costume jewellery of the 19th and 20th centuries from Victoriana to Art Nouveau Art Deco and designers such as Trifari Heskell and Dior up to 1980s Chanel. Price ranges are also covered.

Author: Daniel Goleman
Title: Emotional Intelligence (753292)
ItemDescription: This study argues that "emotional intelligence" is more important than a high IQ in terms of how one performs in life. It claims that qualities such as impulse control persistence zeal self-motivation empathy and social deftness are the characteristics of people who excel.
